description_long: This is a vintage corded telephone with a robust, ornate metal base featuring decorative engravings and brass accents. The main body is rectangular with a circular rotary dial on the front, surrounded by metallic rivets and a central label. The handset is supported by a pair of brass cradles and is connected to the base by a coiled black cord. The earpiece and mouthpiece are both cylindrical and finished in black, matching the cord. The side of the base features a crank handle, indicative of early telephone designs. The overall appearance is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century telephones, combining black lacquered surfaces with polished brass and copper details.
description: An ornate vintage corded telephone with a rotary dial, brass accents, and a coiled cord connecting the handset to the base. The design features decorative engravings and a side crank handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, ornate engravings, and the handset resting on brass cradles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crank handle and the profile of the handset and coiled cord.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset, the coiled cord, and the symmetrical brass supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the coiled cord attachment and the side profile of the rotary dial.
